• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

Multivariate Regression Analysis: 6/2025

Ryan Taylor

8/1/2025

Overview

This document records the methods and results of one multivariate regression analysis and a series of regressions using splines to describe brain volume patterns in craniosynostosis patients.

Load and clean data

First we load the data for the analysis. All of the data is stored in the “Volumetric Results-8-6-2024” Excel file.

We create a clean column indicating the category of suture that we will use in our categorical predictor variable. If a patient has both left coronal and right coronal sutures, we classify them as “Bicoronal.” If they have another combination of multiple sutures, we classify them as “multiple,” and eventually exclude them. If they have no sutures, we classify them as “No suture.” Otherwise, we classify them based on the single suture they have.

here::i_am("analysis/2025.06_cranio_volume_patterns.Rmd")

IMAGES_FOLDER <- here::here("analysis/Presentation Images")

# File saved in "data" folder one level above repo
cranio_raw <- read_xlsx(here::here("../data/VolumetricResults-8-6-2024.xlsx"))

# Rename for easier coding
cranio <- cranio_raw %>% clean_names() %>% 
  rename(head_vol = head_volume_m_l,
         ref_head_vol = personalized_normative_reference_head_volume_m_l,
         age = age_at_image_days) %>% 
  # Rename all "volume_difference" outcome variables to "vol_dev" and shorten "region"
  rename_with(.cols = matches("volume_difference"), 
              ~str_replace(., "volume_difference_m_l", "vol_dev") %>% 
                str_replace("region", "reg")) %>% 
  # Add volume deviation across entire head
  mutate(vol_dev_head = head_vol - ref_head_vol)

# Categorize each patient separately based on sutures 
# **NOTE: These are not categories used in the regression, but it is useful for plotting
# - (creating "bicoronal" / "multiple")
sut <- cranio %>% select(matches("fused")) %>% 
  mutate(i = 1:n()) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = -i) %>%
  mutate(suture = if_else(value == 1, name, NA_character_)) %>% 
  group_by(i) %>% 
  summarize(sutures = paste(na.omit(suture), collapse = "/")) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  arrange(i)

# Merge suture classification into original dataset
cranio %<>% 
  bind_cols(sut %>% select(sutures)) %>% 
  mutate(num_sutures = rowSums(select(., matches("fused"))),
         suture_type = case_when(num_sutures == 2 & 
                                   left_coronal_fused == 1 & 
                                   right_coronal_fused == 1 ~ "bicoronal",
                                 num_sutures > 1 ~ "multiple",
                                 num_sutures == 0 ~ "no_suture",
                                 T ~ sutures)) %>% 
  mutate(suture_type = str_remove_all(suture_type, "_fused"))

Data counts

# Summarize counts of pictures and patients
cran_summ <- cranio %>% 
  summarize("Rows" = n(),
            "Unique Pictures" = n_distinct(image_identifier),
            "Unique Patients" = n_distinct(uid),
            "Craniosynostosis Patients" = n_distinct(uid[suture_type != "no_suture"])) %>% 
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "  ", values_to = " ")

# Print summary table
summ_kable <- kable(cran_summ, caption = "All pictures and patients") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)

summ_kable
All pictures and patients
Rows 2626
Unique Pictures 2626
Unique Patients 2464
Craniosynostosis Patients 746

Data cleaning

Based on summaries, there are some rare categories of data that we should exclude to reduce the variability of the data.

We use one image from each patient, which we choose as the last image taken for each patient. We also exclude multiple sutures and lambdoid sutures. Finally, one patient’s sex is missing, so we exclude that patient.

# Filter to regression subset:
# - Exclude lambdoid
# - Age less than 1 year
# - No missing sex or age
cranio_sub <- cranio %>% 
  # Exclude rare sutures
  filter(!suture_type %in% c("multiple", "left_lambdoid", "right_lambdoid") &
           age <= 365 &
           !is.na(pt_sex) & !is.na(age))

# Format for cross-sectional multivariate reg
cranio_reg <- cranio_sub %>% 
  # Filter to one picture per patient
  arrange(uid, age) %>% 
  group_by(uid) %>% 
  # Use last picture taken for each patient
  slice_tail(n=1) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  # Code suture type as factor based on frequency
  mutate(suture_type_cln = str_replace_all(suture_type, "_", " ") %>% 
           str_to_title(),
         suture_type = fct_infreq(suture_type),
         suture_type_cln = fct_infreq(suture_type_cln))

Clean Summary statistics

Clean data counts

### Summaries for abstract

# Image types
summ_image <- tabyl(cranio_sub, image_type) %>% 
  adorn_totals("row")

kbl(summ_image, caption = "Images by Image Type") %>% kable_styling(full_width = F)
Images by Image Type
image_type n percent
3D photo 313 0.2430124
CT 975 0.7569876
Total 1288 1.0000000 
# Sex
summ_gender <- cranio_reg %>% 
  group_by(pt_sex) %>% 
  summarize(n = n_distinct(uid)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  adorn_totals("row")

kbl(summ_gender, caption = "Patients by Gender") %>% kable_styling(full_width = F)
Patients by Gender
pt_sex n
Female 470
Male 713
Total 1183 
# Age
summ_age <- cranio_sub %>% 
  group_by(uid) %>% 
  slice(1) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  summarize(age_min = min(age),
            age_med = median(age),
            age_max = max(age)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(across(everything(),
                ~. / 30))

kbl(summ_age, caption = "Patients Age Distribution") %>% kable_styling(full_width = F)
Patients Age Distribution
age_min age_med age_max
0 4.033333 12.13333 
### Summarize regression data counts of pictures and patients
cran_reg_summ <- cranio_reg %>% 
  summarize("Rows" = n(),
            "Unique Pictures" = n_distinct(image_identifier),
            "Unique Patients" = n_distinct(uid),
            "Craniosynostosis Patients" = n_distinct(uid[suture_type != "no_suture"])) %>% 
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "  ", values_to = " ")

# Print summary table
summ_reg_kable <- kable(cran_reg_summ, caption = "Regression pictures and patients") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)

summ_reg_kable
Regression pictures and patients
Rows 1183
Unique Pictures 1183
Unique Patients 1183
Craniosynostosis Patients 585

Table 1

With the cleaned data, we start with a Table 1 displaying information about the infants in our sample.

label(cranio_reg$image_type) <- "Image Type"
label(cranio_reg$pt_sex) <- "Sex"
label(cranio_reg$pt_race) <- "Race"
label(cranio_reg$pt_ethnicity) <- "Ethnicity"
label(cranio_reg$age) <- "Age"
label(cranio_reg$head_vol) <- "Overall Head Vol. (mL)"
label(cranio_reg$ref_head_vol) <- "Reference Head Vol. (mL)"



# Create table 1
demo_table <- table1::table1(~ 
                               image_type + pt_sex + pt_race + pt_ethnicity + 
                               age + head_vol + 
                               ref_head_vol | suture_type_cln,
                             data = cranio_reg)

demo_table_l <- t1kable(demo_table, 
                        format = "latex",
                        booktabs = T,
                        label = "t1") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F, latex_options = "hold_position")

demo_table

Explanatory Regression

Next, we run a multivariate regression to find the association between the 12 regions’ volume differences in a given patient with that patient’s suture type.

# Create list of suture type vars
cranio_fusion_vars <- cranio_reg %>% 
  select(matches("_fused")) %>% 
  select(-matches("lambdoid")) %>% 
  names()

# Preserve means and sd's for consistent normalization
cranio_scale <- cranio_reg %>% 
  summarize(across(c(head_vol, vol_dev_head, age),
                   .fns = list("mean" = ~mean(., na.rm = T),
                               "sd" = ~sd(., na.rm = T))))

# Normalize variables
cranio_reg %<>%
  mutate(across(c(head_vol, vol_dev_head, age),
                ~ ( . - cranio_scale[[paste0(cur_column(), "_mean")]][1]) / 
                  cranio_scale[[paste0(cur_column(), "_sd")]][1],
                .names = "{.col}_std"))

# Create regression formula: 
# => bind 12 outcome vars ~ Suture types (inc. bicoronal interaction) & 
#                           Overall head volume dev & Image type & Sex & Age
reg_form <- formula(paste("cbind(",
                          paste(
                            paste0("reg_", 1:12, "_vol_dev"),
                            collapse = ", "),
                          ") ~ ", 
                          paste(cranio_fusion_vars, collapse = " + "), 
                          " + right_coronal_fused * left_coronal_fused",
                          " + vol_dev_head_std + image_type + pt_sex + age_std"))

# Run separate model with only demographics for comparison
demo_form <- formula(paste("cbind(",
                          paste(
                            paste0("reg_", 1:12, "_vol_dev"),
                            collapse = ", "),
                          ") ~ ", 
                          "vol_dev_head_std + image_type + pt_sex + age_std"))

# Run regression
vol_reg <- lm(reg_form, data = cranio_reg)

# Run reduced model (without sutures)
demo_reg <- lm(demo_form, data = cranio_reg)

The results from this model are presented in the table below. We present an estimate of the effects of a bicoronal suture fusion, estimated as the sum of the coefficients of left coronal, right coronal, and their interaction.

# Save regression result in table
beta_tbl <- broom::tidy(vol_reg)

# Clean var-covar matrix for variance estimates
beta_cov <- vcov(vol_reg) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column() %>% 
  separate_wider_delim(rowname, ":", 
                       names = c("row_outcome", "row_var"), 
                       too_many = "merge") %>% 
  pivot_longer(-c(row_outcome, row_var), 
               names_to = "colname") %>% 
  separate_wider_delim(colname, ":", 
                       names = c("col_outcome", "col_var"),
                       too_many = "merge")

# Calculate bicoronal combination variance
vol_vcov <- vcov(vol_reg)

# Identify coronal variables in each region
coronal_ptrn_vec <- paste0("reg_", 1:12, "_.*coronal")

# Create several vectors with 1's in the place of coronal vars
bicoronal_c <- map(coronal_ptrn_vec,
                   ~as.numeric(str_detect(colnames(vol_vcov), .x)))

# Multiply contrast vectors with vcov matrix to get combined variance
bicoronal_var <- tibble(
  response = paste0("reg_", 1:12, "_vol_dev")
) %>% 
  mutate(var = map_dbl(bicoronal_c, 
                       ~t(.) %*% vol_vcov %*% .))
         
# Add better bicoronal interpretation to coefficients table (combining first order and interaction)
bicoronal_tbl <- beta_tbl %>% 
  filter(str_detect(term, "coronal")) %>% 
  group_by(response) %>% 
  summarize(estimate = sum(estimate)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  # Join variance of all coronal-related terms
  # Q: Accurate std error?
  left_join(bicoronal_var, by = "response") %>% 
  mutate(std.error = sqrt(var)) %>% 
  mutate(statistic = estimate / std.error,
         p.value = pnorm(-abs(statistic)),
         term = "bicoronal") %>% 
  select(-var)

# Create full coefficients table
coeff_tbl <- beta_tbl %>% 
  filter(str_detect(term, "fused")) %>% 
  bind_rows(bicoronal_tbl) %>% 
  bind_rows(beta_tbl %>% filter(!str_detect(term, "fused"))) %>% 
  mutate(region_num = str_extract(response, "[0-9]+") %>% 
           as.numeric()) %>% 
  arrange(region_num)
# Extract RSE and R Squared measures for all 12 regions
lm_stats <- distinct(coeff_tbl, region_num) %>% 
  mutate(rse = purrr::map_dbl(1:length(summary(vol_reg)),
                              ~summary(vol_reg)[.][[1]]$sigma),
         r_2 = purrr::map_dbl(1:length(summary(vol_reg)),
                              ~summary(vol_reg)[.][[1]]$r.squared),
         adj_r_2 = purrr::map_dbl(1:length(summary(vol_reg)),
                                  ~summary(vol_reg)[.][[1]]$adj.r.squared),
         adj_r_2_demo = purrr::map_dbl(1:length(summary(demo_reg)),
                                  ~summary(demo_reg)[.][[1]]$adj.r.squared))

# Merge coefficients and R squared for models; calculate confidence intervals
coeff_tbl %<>% left_join(lm_stats) %>% 
  select(response, rse, r_2, adj_r_2, everything()) %>% 
  mutate(low_95 = estimate - 1.96*std.error,
         high_95 = estimate + 1.96*std.error,
         region_num = str_extract(response, "[0-9]+") %>% as.numeric(),
         clean_var = str_remove_all(term, "suture_type"),
         term_name = paste0(region_num, ": ", clean_var))

# Compare full and residual explanatory models
exp_model_comp <- anova(vol_reg, demo_reg, test = "Pillai")

exp_model_comp_sens <- map(c("Pillai", "Wilks", "Hotelling-Lawley", "Roy"),
                           ~anova(vol_reg, demo_reg, test = .x))

# Run separate models for R-squared comparisons
exp_anova_df <- tibble(region_num = 1:12) %>% 
  mutate(reg_form = map_chr(1:12,
                            ~paste(paste0("reg_", .x, "_vol_dev"),
                                   "~", 
                                   paste(cranio_fusion_vars, collapse = " + "), 
                                   " + right_coronal_fused * left_coronal_fused",
                                   " + vol_dev_head_std + image_type + pt_sex + age_std")),
         
         demo_form = map_chr(1:12,
                             ~paste(paste0("reg_", .x, "_vol_dev"),
                                    "~", 
                                    "vol_dev_head_std + image_type + pt_sex + age_std"))) %>% 
  mutate(
    vol_reg = map(reg_form, ~lm(formula(.), data = cranio_reg)),
    demo_reg = map(demo_form, ~lm(formula(.), data = cranio_reg))
  ) %>% 
  mutate(anova_test = map2(vol_reg, demo_reg, ~anova(.x, .y))) %>% 
  mutate(f_stat = map_dbl(anova_test, ~.[["F"]][2]),
         p_value = map_dbl(anova_test, ~.[["Pr(>F)"]][2]))

# Merge F stats
lm_stats %<>% 
  left_join(
    exp_anova_df %>% select(region_num, f_stat, p_value)
            ) %>% 
  mutate(p_value = format.pval(p_value, digits = 2, eps = 0.0001))

# Display using kable, coloring background of each column to show best predictions
lm_stats_k <- lm_stats %>% 
  select(region_num, matches("adj_r_2|f_stat|p_value"))

# Export latex version without coloring
lm_kable_l <- kbl(lm_stats_k, caption = "Model Performance Statistics", digits = 3,
                col.names = c("Region","Adj. R Squared", "Adj. R Sq. w/o Sutures", 
                              "F Statistic", "P Value"), escape = F, booktabs = T,
                format = "latex", label = "explr2", linesep = "") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)

r_2_range <- lm_stats %>% 
  select(matches("adj_r_2")) %>% 
  pivot_longer(everything()) %$% 
  unique(value)

lm_stats_k$adj_r_2 <- cell_spec(round(lm_stats$adj_r_2, 3), color = "black", bold = T, 
                                background = spec_color(lm_stats$adj_r_2, 
                                                        option = "turbo",
                                                        scale_from = c(min(r_2_range), max(r_2_range)),
                                                        begin = 0.5, end = 0.8, 
                                                        direction = -1))

lm_stats_k$adj_r_2_demo <- cell_spec(round(lm_stats$adj_r_2_demo, 3), color = "black", bold = T, 
                                     background = spec_color(lm_stats$adj_r_2_demo, 
                                                             option = "turbo",
                                                             scale_from = c(min(r_2_range), max(r_2_range)),
                                                             begin = 0.5, end = 0.8, 
                                                        direction = -1))

lm_stats_k$f_stat <- cell_spec(round(lm_stats$f_stat, 1), color = "black", 
                                     background = spec_color(lm_stats$f_stat, 
                                                             option = "turbo",
                                                             begin = 0.5, end = 0.8, 
                                                        direction = -1))

# Create table
lm_kable <- kbl(lm_stats_k, caption = "Model Performance Statistics", digits = 3,
                col.names = c("Region","Adj. R Squared", "Adj. R Sq. w/o Sutures", 
                              "F Statistic", "P Value"), escape = F) %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)

# Print table
lm_kable
Model Performance Statistics
Region Adj. R Squared Adj. R Sq. w/o Sutures F Statistic P Value
1 0.5 0.354 69.5 <0.0001
2 0.438 0.217 93.7 <0.0001
3 0.432 0.199 98 <0.0001
4 0.552 0.39 85.9 <0.0001
5 0.56 0.377 98.7 <0.0001
6 0.784 0.628 170.8 <0.0001
7 0.345 0.222 45.3 <0.0001
8 0.515 0.374 69.7 <0.0001
9 0.396 0.288 43 <0.0001
10 0.357 0.176 67.4 <0.0001
11 0.79 0.621 190.2 <0.0001
12 0.601 0.289 184.8 <0.0001

Coefficients on the brain

We create a brain shape to plot the results on top of the region map:

# Include diagram from Porras paper
knitr::include_graphics(here::here("../Literature/Colorado/Region_Diagram.png"))

# Manually define center points of regions
brain_points <- tribble(
  ~region, ~x, ~y,
  1, 1, 7,
  2, 2, 8, 
  3, 3, 8,
  4, 4, 7,
  5, 4, 5,
  6, 3, 4, 
  7, 4, 3,
  8, 3, 1,
  9, 2, 1,
  10, 1, 3,
  11, 2, 4,
  12, 1, 5
) %>% 
  st_as_sf(coords = c("x", "y"), remove = F)

# Create nearest-neighbor regions around these points
g = st_combine(st_geometry(brain_points)) # make multipoint
v = st_voronoi(g)
v = st_collection_extract(v)

# Add region numbers
brain_shapes <- v[unlist(st_intersects(brain_points, v))] %>% 
  st_as_sf() %>% 
  mutate(region_num = 1:n())

# Create circle around whole shape
brain_circle <- tibble(x = 2.5, y = 4.5) %>% 
  st_as_sf(coords = c("x", "y")) %>% 
  st_buffer(4.5)

# Crop nearest neighbor regions to circle
brain_shapes <- st_intersection(brain_shapes, brain_circle)

Next, we plot the coefficients on the map of the brain regions.

# Extract and clean coefficients and names
reg_coeffs <- coeff_tbl %>% 
  mutate(var_clean = str_remove_all(term, "_fused") %>% 
           str_replace_all("_", " ") %>% 
           str_remove_all("\\(|\\)") %>% 
           str_to_title()) %>%
  mutate(var_clean = case_match(var_clean,
                                "Vol Dev Head Std" ~ "Actual - Ref (Std.)", 
                                "Image Typect" ~ "CT vs 3D",
                                "Pt Sexmale" ~ "Sex: Male",
                                "Age Std" ~ "Age (Std.)",
                                .default = var_clean)) %>% 
  mutate(var_clean = fct_inorder(var_clean)) %>% 
  left_join(brain_shapes) %>% 
  st_as_sf()

# Filter out bicoronal interaction term
reg_coeffs %<>% 
  filter(!term %in% c("left_coronal_fused:right_coronal_fused"))

# Identify suture vs demographics
reg_coeffs %<>%
  mutate(is_suture = if_else(str_detect(term, "fused|bicoronal"), 1, 0))

reg_coeffs_control <- reg_coeffs %>% 
  filter(is_suture == 0)

reg_coeffs_fusion <- reg_coeffs %>% 
  filter(is_suture == 1)

# Plot coefficients on brain shape
coeff_plot_control <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = reg_coeffs_control, 
          aes(fill = estimate)) +
  geom_sf_text(data = st_centroid(reg_coeffs_control), aes(label = region_num)) +
  facet_wrap(~var_clean, nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "turbo", 
                       limits = (c(-1, 1) * max(abs(reg_coeffs$estimate)))) +
  labs(x = "", y = "", fill = "Estimate") +
  theme_void()

coeff_plot_fusion <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = reg_coeffs_fusion, aes(fill = estimate)) +
  geom_sf_text(data = st_centroid(reg_coeffs_fusion), aes(label = region_num)) +
  facet_wrap(~var_clean, nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "turbo", 
                       limits = (c(-1, 1) * max(abs(reg_coeffs$estimate)))) +
  labs(x = "", y = "", fill = "Estimate") +
  theme_void()

coeff_plot_all0 <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = reg_coeffs, aes(fill = estimate)) +
  geom_sf_text(data = st_centroid(reg_coeffs), aes(label = region_num)) +
  facet_wrap(~var_clean, nrow = 3) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "turbo", 
                       limits = (c(-1, 1) * max(abs(reg_coeffs$estimate)))) +
  labs(x = "", y = "", fill = "Estimate") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.direction = "horizontal")

coeff_plot_all <- shift_legend2(coeff_plot_all0)

coeff_plot_control

coeff_plot_fusion

coeff_plot_all
## TableGrob (27 x 25) "layout": 85 grobs
##     z         cells             name
## 1   1 (10-10, 7- 7)        panel-1-1
## 2   1 (15-15, 7- 7)        panel-2-1
## 3   1 (20-20, 7- 7)        panel-3-1
## 4   1 (10-10,11-11)        panel-4-1
## 5   1 (15-15,11-11)        panel-1-2
## 6   1 (20-20,11-11)        panel-2-2
## 7   1 (10-10,15-15)        panel-3-2
## 8   1 (15-15,15-15)        panel-4-2
## 9   1 (20-20,15-15)        panel-1-3
## 10  1 (10-10,19-19)        panel-2-3
## 11  1 (15-15,19-19)        panel-3-3
## 12  1 (20-20,19-19)        panel-4-3

ggsave(paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/coeff_plot_control.png"),
       coeff_plot_control,
       width = 8, height = 4, units = "in")

ggsave(paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/coeff_plot_fusion.png"),
       coeff_plot_fusion,
       width = 8, height = 4, units = "in")

ggsave(paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/coeff_plot_all.png"),
       coeff_plot_all,
       width = 5, height = 4, units = "in")

Predictive Model

Cross-Validated Predictions

We create a fresh version of the “AR” data, including information from previous visits, in order to keep this data not reliant on the above sections.

# Create new data with previous values for cross-validated model
cranio_cv <- cranio_sub %>% 
  # Enforce order of patient IDs
  arrange(uid, age) %>% 
  group_by(uid) %>% 
  mutate(pt_visits = n(),
         visit = 1:n(),
         across(c(age, vol_dev_head, matches("reg_")),
                .fns = list("prev" = ~lag(., 1),
                            "diff" = ~ . - lag(., 1)))) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(across(c(head_vol, vol_dev_head, age),
                ~ ( . - cranio_scale[[paste0(cur_column(), "_mean")]][1]) / 
                  cranio_scale[[paste0(cur_column(), "_sd")]][1],
                .names = "{.col}_std"))

# Limit data to one observation per patient (all patients)
cranio_cv_1per <- cranio_cv %>% 
  group_by(uid) %>% 
  # Use last picture taken for each patient
  slice_tail(n=1) %>% 
  ungroup()

# Keep 2nd visit for each patient
cranio_cv_mult <- cranio_cv %>% 
  filter(visit == 2) %>% 
  # Exclude only observation of their suture type
  group_by(suture_type) %>% 
  mutate(n_obs = n()) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  filter(n_obs > 1) %>% 
  select(-n_obs) %>% 
  # Code suture type as factor based on frequency
  mutate(suture_type_cln = str_replace_all(suture_type, "_", " ") %>% 
           str_to_title(),
         suture_type = fct_infreq(suture_type),
         suture_type_cln = fct_infreq(suture_type_cln))

# Create wider data with interactions between covariates and regions
cranio_cv_comb <- cranio_cv_mult %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("reg_"),
               names_pattern = "(reg_[0-9]+)_(.*)",
               names_to = c("region", ".value")) %>% 
  mutate(image_type = as.numeric(image_type == "CT"),
         pt_sex = as.numeric(pt_sex == "Male"),
         region2 = region) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = region,
              values_from = c(matches("fused"), 
                              image_type, pt_sex, age_std, vol_dev_head_prev), 
              names_glue = "{region}_{.value}", 
              values_fill = 0) %>% 
  select(uid, age, region = region2,
         matches("reg_"), vol_dev_prev, vol_dev, age_diff) %>% 
  select(-matches("lambdoid"))

Longitudinal Data Summaries

# Keep all images from patients with multiple visits

cranio_cv_longit <- cranio_cv %>% 
  filter(pt_visits > 1)

# Summarize counts in multiple visit data
ar_summ <- cranio_cv_longit %>% 
  summarize("Rows" = n(),
            "Unique Pictures" = n_distinct(image_identifier),
            "Unique Patients" = n_distinct(uid)) %>% 
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "  ", values_to = " ")

kable(ar_summ, caption = "Repeat Visits Data") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)
Repeat Visits Data
Rows 191
Unique Pictures 191
Unique Patients 86 
# Sex
summ_gender_mult <- cranio_cv_longit %>% 
  group_by(pt_sex) %>% 
  summarize(n = n_distinct(uid)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  adorn_totals("row")

kbl(summ_gender_mult, caption = "Longitudinal Patients by Gender") %>% kable_styling(full_width = F)
Longitudinal Patients by Gender
pt_sex n
Female 24
Male 62
Total 86 
# Age
summ_age_cv <- cranio_cv_longit %>% 
  filter(visit == 1) %>% 
  summarize(age_min = min(age),
            age_med = median(age),
            age_max = max(age)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(across(everything(),
                ~. / 30))

kbl(summ_age_cv, caption = "Longitudinal Patients Age Distibution") %>% kable_styling(full_width = F)
Longitudinal Patients Age Distibution
age_min age_med age_max
0.0333333 3.866667 10.36667 
# Pics per patient
summ_pics_cv <- cranio_cv_longit %>% 
  summarize(pics_min = min(pt_visits),
            pics_med = median(pt_visits),
            pics_max = max(pt_visits)) %>% 
  ungroup()

kbl(summ_pics_cv, caption = "Longitudinal Patients Pictures Distibution") %>% kable_styling(full_width = F)
Longitudinal Patients Pictures Distibution
pics_min pics_med pics_max
2 2 5 
# Time between
summ_betw_cv <- cranio_cv_mult %>% 
  summarize(time_min = min(age_diff, na.rm = T),
            time_med = median(age_diff, na.rm = T),
            time_max = max(age_diff, na.rm = T)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(across(everything(),
                ~. / 30))

kbl(summ_betw_cv, caption = "Longitudinal Patients Interval Distibution") %>% kable_styling(full_width = F)
Longitudinal Patients Interval Distibution
time_min time_med time_max
0.0333333 1.866667 8.833333 
# Summarize suture types in multiple visit data
sut_ar <- cranio_cv_mult %>% 
  group_by(suture_type) %>% 
  summarize(pics = n(),
            patients = n_distinct(uid)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  arrange(desc(pics)) %>% 
  mutate(pats_included = if_else(suture_type %in% c("multiple", "right_lambdoid", "left_lambdoid"), 0, patients),
         pct_pats = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)(patients / sum(patients)),
         pct_included = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)(pats_included / sum(pats_included))) %>% 
  adorn_totals("row") %>% 
  select(-pats_included)

sut_kable_ar <- kable(sut_ar, caption = "Sutures: Predictive Data", digits = 2,
      col.names = c("Suture Type", "Pictures", "Patients", "% of Patients", "% of Included")) %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F)

sut_kable_ar
Sutures: Predictive Data
Suture Type Pictures Patients % of Patients % of Included
sagittal 55 55 64.7% 64.7%
metopic 20 20 23.5% 23.5%
right_coronal 7 7 8.2% 8.2%
bicoronal 3 3 3.5% 3.5%
Total 85 85 
label(cranio_cv_mult$image_type) <- "Image Type"
label(cranio_cv_mult$pt_sex) <- "Sex"
label(cranio_cv_mult$pt_race) <- "Race"
label(cranio_cv_mult$pt_ethnicity) <- "Ethnicity"
label(cranio_cv_mult$age) <- "Age"
label(cranio_cv_mult$head_vol) <- "Overall Head Vol. (mL)"
label(cranio_cv_mult$ref_head_vol) <- "Reference Head Vol. (mL)"

# Create table 1
demo_table_ar <- table1::table1(~ 
                               image_type + pt_sex + pt_race + pt_ethnicity + 
                               age + head_vol + 
                               ref_head_vol | suture_type_cln,
                             data = cranio_cv_mult)

demo_table_ar_l <- t1kable(demo_table_ar, 
                        format = "latex",
                        booktabs = T,
                        label = "t1ar") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width = F, latex_options = "hold_position")

3D photo 43 (78.2%) 17 (85.0%) 6 (85.7%) 1 (33.3%) 67 (78.8%)

Female 13 (23.6%) 4 (20.0%) 5 (71.4%) 1 (33.3%) 23 (27.1%)
Male 42 (76.4%) 16 (80.0%) 2 (28.6%) 2 (66.7%) 62 (72.9%)

African or African American 1 (1.8%) 0 (0%) 1 (14.3%) 0 (0%) 2 (2.4%)

Asian 6 (10.9%) 3 (15.0%) 0 (0%) 1 (33.3%) 10 (11.8%)

Mean (SD) 171 (79.8) 222 (80.1) 206 (61.3) 181 (160) 186 (83.2)
Median [Min, Max] 154 [48.0, 360] 218 [88.0, 340] 199 [131, 274] 184 [20.0, 340] 181 [20.0, 360]

Mean (SD) 714 (173) 847 (174) 692 (144) 718 (247) 744 (180)

Mean (SD) 697 (157) 817 (149) 677 (124) 684 (239) 723 (162)
Median [Min, Max] 685 [376, 1050] 792 [570, 1060] 611 [591, 943] 731 [425, 896] 716 [376, 1060]

CV Predictions

We run a loop estimating several models while excluding one patient who has multiple visits. Then, we predict using that model on the excluded patient to determine that model’s overall accuracy.

# Define formula for combined spline model
reg_form_combo <- formula(
    paste0("vol_dev ~ 0 + te(age_diff, vol_dev_prev) + ",
           paste(names(cranio_cv_comb %>% 
                         select(matches("reg_"))),
                 collapse = " + "), " + ",
           paste(paste0("reg_", 1:12, 
                        "_left_coronal_fused * reg_", 1:12, 
                        "_right_coronal_fused"), collapse = " + ")
    ))

### Initialize loop

# Save set of patients with multiple visits
subj_ids_mult <- unique(cranio_cv_mult$uid)

# Count patients with multiple visits
n_subj_mult <- length(subj_ids_mult)

# - Mean Sq Pred Error
# - Prediction plots (brain shape for all 88)

# Initialize results data
CV_results <- tibble(NULL)

subj_i <- 1
for(subj_i in 1:n_subj_mult){
  
  # Pull current subject to be left out
  cur_subj <- subj_ids_mult[subj_i]
  
  # Drop subject from all data
  cranio_cv_drop_i <- cranio_cv_1per %>% 
    filter(uid != cur_subj)
  
  # Drop subject from only repeat (2nd) visits
  cranio_cv_mult_drop_i <- cranio_cv_mult %>% 
    filter(uid != cur_subj)
  
  # Drop subject for combined model data
  cranio_cv_comb_drop_i <- cranio_cv_comb %>% 
    filter(uid != cur_subj)
  
  # Keep separate data of dropped subject
  cranio_cv_mult_i <- cranio_cv_mult %>% 
    filter(uid == cur_subj)
  
  # Keep separate combined data of dropped subject
  cranio_cv_comb_i <- cranio_cv_comb %>% 
    filter(uid == cur_subj)
  
  ### Fit models:
  
  # 1) Data of last / only visits (model not relying on previous)
  all_pts_models_drop_i <- cranio_cv_drop_i %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("reg_"),
                 names_pattern = "(reg_[0-9]+)_(.*)",
                 names_to = c("region", ".value")) %>%
    group_by(region) %>%
    nest() %>%
    # Linear model
    mutate(
      simple_all_model = map(data, 
                         ~lm(vol_dev ~ 0 +
                               metopic_fused + sagittal_fused + 
                               left_coronal_fused + right_coronal_fused + 
                               right_coronal_fused * left_coronal_fused + 
                               image_type + pt_sex + age_std,
                             data = .x)))
  
  # 2) Data excluding first / only visits (models relying on previous visits)
  rpt_pts_models_drop_i <- cranio_cv_mult_drop_i %>% 
    pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("reg_"),
                 names_pattern = "(reg_[0-9]+)_(.*)",
                 names_to = c("region", ".value")) %>%
    group_by(region) %>%
    nest() %>%
    mutate(
      simple_rpt_model = map(data, 
                             ~lm(vol_dev ~ 0 +
                                   metopic_fused + sagittal_fused + 
                                   left_coronal_fused + right_coronal_fused + 
                                   right_coronal_fused * left_coronal_fused + 
                                   image_type + pt_sex + age_std,
                                 data = .x)),
      # Linear model using previous observations for region and overall
      prev_model = map(data,
                       ~lm(vol_dev ~ 0 +
                             metopic_fused + sagittal_fused + 
                             left_coronal_fused + right_coronal_fused + 
                             right_coronal_fused * left_coronal_fused + 
                             image_type + pt_sex + age_std + 
                             vol_dev_head_prev + vol_dev_prev, 
                           data = .x)),
      # GAM using linear previous overall and surface of previous region
      te_model = map(data,
                     ~mgcv::gam(vol_dev ~ 0 +
                                  metopic_fused + sagittal_fused + 
                                  left_coronal_fused + right_coronal_fused + 
                                  right_coronal_fused * left_coronal_fused + 
                                  image_type + pt_sex + age_std + 
                                  vol_dev_head_prev +
                                  te(age_diff, vol_dev_prev), 
                                data = .x))
    )
  
  # 3) "Combined" data: excluding first / only visits, with region interactions
  comb_model_drop_i <- mgcv::gam(reg_form_combo, 
                                 data = cranio_cv_comb_drop_i)
  
  # Get region predictions for excluded observation
  region_models_pred <- cranio_cv_mult_i %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("reg_"),
                 names_pattern = "(reg_[0-9]+)_(.*)",
                 names_to = c("region", ".value")) %>%
    group_by(region) %>%
    nest(.key = "new_sample") %>%
    # Merge models estimated without observation
    left_join(all_pts_models_drop_i, join_by(region)) %>% 
    rename(all_pts_data = data) %>% 
    left_join(rpt_pts_models_drop_i, join_by(region)) %>% 
    mutate(simple_all_pred = map2_dbl(simple_all_model, new_sample, 
                                      ~predict(.x, newdata = .y)),
           simple_rpt_pred = map2_dbl(simple_rpt_model, new_sample, 
                                      ~predict(.x, newdata = .y)),
           prev_pred = map2_dbl(prev_model, new_sample, 
                                ~predict(.x, newdata = .y)),
           te_pred = map2_dbl(te_model, new_sample, 
                              ~predict(.x, newdata = .y))) %>%
    hoist(new_sample, "vol_dev") %>% 
    hoist(new_sample, "vol_dev_prev")
  
  # Predict combined model on 1 observation's 12 outcomes
  cranio_cv_comb_i$combo_pred <- predict(comb_model_drop_i,
                                         newdata = cranio_cv_comb_i)
  
  # Compile original values and predictions from every model
  pred_df_i <- region_models_pred %>% 
    select(region, vol_dev, vol_dev_prev, matches("_pred")) %>% 
    ungroup() %>% 
    # Merge predictions from combined model
    left_join(cranio_cv_comb_i %>% 
                select(region, combo_pred),
              join_by(region)) %>% 
    # Calculate out of sample accuracy
    mutate(across(matches("_pred"),
                  ~ (vol_dev - .)^2,
                  .names = "{.col}_sqerr"))
  
  # Add to running total of results
  new_rows <- pred_df_i %>% 
    mutate(
      dropped_subject = subj_i,
      dropped_subject_id = cur_subj,
    )
  
  # Save in full results data
  CV_results <- bind_rows(CV_results, new_rows)
}

CV Prediction Summaries

We summarize the distributions of square errors from these predictions. We want to look at how the various models may perform better or worse across suture types and individual regions.

# Save info about patients to look for patterns
patient_info <- cranio_cv_1per %>% 
  select(uid, pt_sex, pt_race, pt_ethnicity, suture_type)

# Count number of observations of each suture type
sut_counts_cv <- cranio_cv_mult %>% 
  group_by(suture_type) %>% 
  summarize(sut_obs = n()) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(sut_pct = sut_obs / sum(sut_obs))

# Merge this info to predictions
cv_out <- CV_results %>% 
  left_join(patient_info, by = c("dropped_subject_id" = "uid")) %>% 
  left_join(sut_counts_cv, by = "suture_type") %>% 
  mutate(region_num = as.numeric(str_extract(region, "[0-9]+"))) %>% 
  # Code suture type as factor based on frequency
  mutate(suture_type_cln = str_replace_all(suture_type, "_", " ") %>% 
           str_to_title(),
         suture_type = fct_infreq(suture_type),
         suture_type_cln = fct_infreq(suture_type_cln))

# Pivot so that model name is in a column
cv_out_long <- cv_out %>% 
  rename(vol_dev_pred = vol_dev,
         vol_dev_prev_pred = vol_dev_prev) %>% 
  pivot_longer(matches("_pred"), 
               names_pattern = "(.*)_(pred.*)",
               names_to = c("model", ".value")) %>% 
  mutate(pred_abserr = sqrt(pred_sqerr))

# Create pretty names for models
cv_out_long %<>%
  mutate(model = factor(model, levels = c("vol_dev", "vol_dev_prev", "simple_all", "simple_rpt", "prev", "te", "combo"))) %>% 
  arrange(model) %>% 
  mutate(model_neat = case_match(model, 
                                 "vol_dev" ~ "Truth",
                                 "vol_dev_prev" ~ "Prev. Visit",
                                 "simple_all" ~ "Control (All Data)",
                                 "simple_rpt" ~ "Model 1",
                                 "prev" ~ "Model 2",
                                 "te" ~ "Model 3",
                                 "combo" ~ "Model 4")) %>% 
  mutate(model_neat = fct_inorder(model_neat))

# Summarize absolute errors by model by suture
err_summ <- cv_out_long %>% 
  filter(!model %in% c("vol_dev", "vol_dev_prev", "simple_all")) %>% 
  # Average across regions to get one square error per patient
  group_by(dropped_subject, dropped_subject_id, 
           suture_type_cln, sut_obs, sut_pct, model_neat) %>%
  summarize(sqerr = mean(pred_sqerr),
            abserr = mean(pred_abserr)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  # Summarize distributions of square errors by suture by model
  group_by(suture_type_cln, sut_obs, sut_pct,
           model_neat) %>% 
  summarize(mean = mean(abserr),
            quant = list(quantile(abserr, 
                                  c(0.05, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.95),
                                  names = T))) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  unnest_wider(quant, names_sep = "_", names_repair = "universal") %>% 
  # Sort by frequency and by best median square error
  arrange(desc(sut_obs), quant_50.)
  
# Display results
kbl(err_summ, 
    caption = "Cross-Validated Absolute Error Summary",
    digits = 3,
    col.names = c("Suture", "Suture Count", "Suture Pct",
                  "Model",
                  "Mean Sq. Error", "5th Pctile", "25th Pctile", "Median", "75th Pctile", "95th Pctile")) %>% 
  kable_styling()
Cross-Validated Absolute Error Summary
Suture Suture Count Suture Pct Model Mean Sq. Error 5th Pctile 25th Pctile Median 75th Pctile 95th Pctile
Sagittal 55 0.647 Model 4 3.942 1.182 1.965 3.157 5.058 8.160
Sagittal 55 0.647 Model 2 3.915 1.157 2.104 3.250 4.655 8.550
Sagittal 55 0.647 Model 1 3.950 1.367 1.840 3.351 4.855 9.049
Sagittal 55 0.647 Model 3 4.162 1.381 2.252 3.412 5.150 8.907
Metopic 20 0.235 Model 4 4.131 1.778 2.512 3.336 5.269 7.910
Metopic 20 0.235 Model 2 4.112 1.828 2.619 3.493 5.024 6.732
Metopic 20 0.235 Model 1 4.284 2.137 2.984 3.523 5.450 6.811
Metopic 20 0.235 Model 3 4.235 2.126 2.453 3.666 5.358 6.502
Right Coronal 7 0.082 Model 1 3.748 1.666 2.160 2.967 4.026 7.783
Right Coronal 7 0.082 Model 3 5.430 2.254 3.589 3.927 6.672 10.991
Right Coronal 7 0.082 Model 2 4.320 2.325 2.944 3.964 4.272 8.023
Right Coronal 7 0.082 Model 4 4.572 2.449 3.201 4.212 4.922 8.054
Bicoronal 3 0.035 Model 1 13.256 9.213 10.949 13.120 15.495 17.395
Bicoronal 3 0.035 Model 4 12.877 8.347 10.493 13.177 15.410 17.196
Bicoronal 3 0.035 Model 2 12.926 8.674 10.746 13.336 15.311 16.892
Bicoronal 3 0.035 Model 3 13.149 8.901 11.012 13.650 15.536 17.045 
# Summarize by model
err_summ_model <- err_summ %>% 
  group_by(model_neat) %>% 
  summarize(model_med = weighted.mean(quant_50., sut_obs)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  arrange(model_med)

# No regions: keep suture together
suture_box <- cv_out_long %>% 
  filter(!model %in% c("vol_dev", "vol_dev_prev", "simple_all")) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = model_neat, 
             y = pred_abserr, 
             fill = model_neat)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~suture_type_cln, nrow = 1) +
  scale_y_continuous(transform = "log", breaks = c(10^c(-3:3))) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom") +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo", begin = 0.2, end = 0.8, direction = -1) +
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = "Out of Sample Prediction Error",
       fill = "Model")

suture_box

ggsave(paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/pred_box_oos.png"),
       suture_box,
       width = 6, height = 4, units = "in")

We also plot a sample of predictions on top of the brain shapes. For each patient, we again take an average of the square errors across all regions for each model. Then, we identify the patients who represent the best or worst overall predictions for each model. For each of those patients, we plot the actual volume deviations and the predictions from every model. Green text and lines indicates the best prediction for a given suture type; pink represents the worst prediction for a given suture type.

# Identify best and worst predictions for each suture
pred_eg <- cv_out_long %>% 
  filter(!model %in% c("simple_all")) %>% 
  group_by(dropped_subject, model) %>% 
  # Average prediction err across regions for each subject and model
  mutate(subj_model_avg_err = mean(pred_sqerr, na.rm = T)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  group_by(suture_type_cln) %>% 
  # Maximum and minimum error for each suture type
  mutate(sut_min_err = min(subj_model_avg_err, na.rm = T),
         sut_max_err = max(subj_model_avg_err, na.rm = T)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(is_min = if_else(is.na(subj_model_avg_err) | 
                            subj_model_avg_err != sut_min_err, 0, 1),
         is_max = if_else(is.na(subj_model_avg_err) | 
                            subj_model_avg_err != sut_max_err, 0, 1)) %>% 
  mutate(example_cat = case_when(is_min == 1 ~ "Best",
                                 is_max == 1 ~ "Worst",
                                 T ~ "Neither") %>% 
           factor(levels = c("Best", "Worst", "Neither"))) %>% 
  # Identify which patient was the min (edit: not the max)
  group_by(dropped_subject) %>% 
  filter(sum(is_min) > 0) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  # Add brain shapes
  left_join(brain_shapes) %>% 
  st_as_sf() %>% 
  # Identify why this patient was included
  mutate(obs_label = case_when(is_min == 1 ~ paste("Min", suture_type_cln, model),
                               is_max == 1 ~ paste("min", suture_type_cln, model))) %>% 
  group_by(dropped_subject) %>% 
  mutate(subj_label = paste(unique(na.omit(obs_label)), collapse = "; "))

# Define range of absolute predictions for plots
pred_range <- c(-1, 1) * max(abs(cv_out_long$pred))

# Plot predictions for each model for each of these examples
pred_plot <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = pred_eg, aes(fill = pred)) +
  geom_sf_text(data = st_centroid(pred_eg), 
               aes(label = region_num),
               show.legend = F, size = 2) +
  facet_grid(suture_type_cln + dropped_subject ~ model_neat,
             switch = "y",
             labeller = label_wrap_gen(10)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "turbo",
                       begin = 0.1, end = 0.9,
                       limits = pred_range,
                       transform = scales::transform_asinh(),
                       breaks = c(-50, -10, -1, 0, 1, 10, 50)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, 
       fill = "Deviation\nPrediction") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 90),
        legend.position = "bottom")

pred_plot_ln <- ggdraw(pred_plot) + 
  draw_line(x = c(0.435, 0.435), y = c(0.15, 0.99),
            color = viridis::turbo(1))

pred_plot_ln

# Save this
ggsave(file = paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/prediction_grid.png"),
       pred_plot_ln,
       height = 3, width = 5, units = "in")

### Also plot a single patient as a descriptive example
pred_eg_lone <- pred_eg %>% 
  filter(dropped_subject == pred_eg$dropped_subject[1])

pred_plot_lone <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = pred_eg_lone, aes(fill = pred)) +
  geom_sf_text(data = st_centroid(pred_eg_lone), 
               aes(label = region_num)) +
  facet_grid(suture_type + dropped_subject ~ model_neat,
             switch = "y",
             labeller = label_wrap_gen(10)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "turbo", limits = pred_range) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, 
       fill = "Prediction", color = "Suture Pred Cat") +
  theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 90))

ggsave(file = paste0(IMAGES_FOLDER, "/prediction_grid_solo.png"),
       pred_plot_lone,
       height = 4, width = 8, units = "in")
